summary:
Free-moving diarthrodial joints have a wide range of motions across various axes while fixed or slightly mobile joints do not offer comparable flexibility. Thus, they display the highest level of movement possible amongst these classifications. Answer: B
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two concise sentences focusing only on critical information for answering about maximum joint movement:

The free-moving diarthrotic joints provide significantly more movement than other classified joints such as amphiarthrosis and syndiathrosis that either limit or barely enable movement at all. Hence, option representing fully moveable joints would be correctly identified here. 

This captures core concepts without extraneous details but still conveys essential distinctions needed to identify where maximal joint activity occurs based given options/types described. Let me know if you need any clarification!
